originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 24: `int codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send` in ak4642_init() (compiled when SOC_AK4642=T). codec_hw_write is assigned but never read — dead store. I2C=T ensures i2c_master_send is declared. FSI_AK4642=T implies SOC_AK4642=T (consistent), FSI_DA7210=T implies SOC_DA7210=T (consistent). No contradictions in PC. The '// ERROR' at L24 marks the VBDB-known I2C=F compile error; this DEAD_STORE (I2C=T) is a distinct new bug not in VBDB. Required condition: SOC_AK4642=T AND I2C=T. PC adds FSI_AK4642=T, FSI_DA7210=T, SOC_DA7210=T — all unnecessary for ak4642_init()'s dead store. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
